item.title: FirstFT: Reform UK surges in early election results
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US-Iran truce strained and ‘potential’ EU talks with Putin

item.title: Big money, hot markets and ‘blissful ignorance’ at Milken
item.description: Financiers convening in Beverly Hills were brimming with optimism in spite of the Iran war and stress in private markets

item.title: Trump’s World Cup chief defends high ticket prices
item.description: ‘We don’t really believe in price controls’ says Andrew Giuliani, despite outcry from fan groups

item.title: US and South Africa hold talks on mining deals after year of tensions
item.description: Officials discussed resources investments as Washington tries to break China’s control of critical minerals production

item.title: US businesses urge Trump to intervene over new EU consumer rules
item.description: American companies fear an update to Brussels’ ‘Product Liability Directive’ will make it easier for consumers to sue

item.title: The jury is still out on AI in finance
item.description: Recruiting digital natives with critical thinking skills is going to be crucial

item.title: Big Tech’s $725bn AI spending spree sends free cash flow to a decade low
item.description: Silicon Valley giants have transformed from asset-light cash machines to huge infrastructure investors

item.title: Anthropic weighs deal for near $1tn valuation as revenue surges
item.description: Start-up behind Claude tool is fielding inbound investment offers that could lead to it surpassing rival OpenAI in value

item.title: Trump sons back $1bn vehicle targeting sectors championed by US president
item.description: Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr invest in entity channelling money into companies in AI and drone sectors

item.title: Billionaire James Packer among backers lined up for OnlyFans deal
item.description: Australian media mogul’s funds to invest in Architect Capital SPV taking a minority stake in platform

item.title: How GSK is rebuilding its cancer business
item.description: A decade after exiting oncology, the UK pharma group is betting that R&D and acquisitions will help it hit sales of £40bn

item.title: GameStop’s wild bid for eBay
item.description: CEO Ryan Cohen is pushing to acquire eBay despite it having a market capitalisation four times that of his company

item.title: Macquarie’s commodities boss paid more than CEO as profits soar
item.description: Simon Wright awarded A$35mn annual pay as energy markets drive bumper returns at ‘millionaires’ factory’

item.title: Paying with your face will become mainstream, says Korean fintech group
item.description: Toss aims to ‘eliminate physical credit cards’ in South Korea in three years

item.title: Multinational back offices choke India’s IT services
item.description: Also in this newsletter: government steps in to help war-affected companies

item.title: Tech boom brings emerging markets and their rich cousins closer together
item.description: Asian manufacturers supplying the picks and shovels of the AI bonanza could be on a more durable footing

item.title: ERG faces semi-nationalisation in boardroom showdown
item.description: Kazakh government official to be proposed as new chief executive in upcoming vote at privately owned mining group

item.title: Trump’s 10% global tariff ruled illegal by US court
item.description: US Court of International Trade says levies applied under Section 122 of trade act are ‘unauthorized by law’

item.title: The Buy America trade
item.description: The rebound is real. Will it last?

item.title: US fund threatens to divest TotalEnergies stake over offshore wind exit
item.description: Symbolic move indicates growing resistance to Trump administration’s payments to stop such projects

item.title: ‘Blissful ignorance’: Milken elite bask in glow of roaring markets
item.description: Financiers at the Beverly Hills gathering brush aside concerns over Iran conflict and private market strains

item.title: Trump’s tariffs and troop cuts could hurt America more than Europe
item.description: As the White House lashes out against Germany, US allies must get serious about strategic autonomy

item.title: IMF warns new AI models risk ‘systemic’ shock to finance
item.description: Fund says preparations needed for ‘inevitable’ AI-enabled breaches of financial institutions’ cyber defences

item.title: Canadian miner Sherritt pulls out of Cuba on Trump sanctions fears
item.description: Termination of 32-year joint venture is a blow to communist island’s economy and could push up cobalt prices

item.title: Old IT makes its bid for AI relevance
item.description: Pendulum is swinging back to companies in areas such as servers, more general chips and software

item.title: Why software firms are calling time on the SaaSpocalypse
item.description: SaaS companies can stay relevant by housing customer data and layering AI on top

item.title: David Attenborough: nature’s great communicator
item.description: At 100, his work should inspire a new generation of scientific truth-tellers

item.title: Chinese overseas M&A hits 5-year high despite regulatory barriers
item.description: Offshore transactions worth nearly $10bn in first quarter as companies target foreign resources, consumer and manufacturing sectors

item.title: Polymarket anonymity must end
item.description: Without identity checks, prediction market regulation will be toothless

item.title: British-Chinese nationals found guilty of spying for Hong Kong
item.description: Two men, including former Border Force official, convicted in trial at London’s Old Bailey
